services.usageSource: gh repo view Aneety/ai --json visibility,isPrivate; gh run list --repo Aneety/ai --limit 200 --json createdAt,updatedAt,status,conclusion,event,workflowName

freeAllowance.label: standard GitHub-hosted runners in public repositories

services.calculation: Aneety/ai visibility is PUBLIC. 100.25 observed minutes in the latest 200 GitHub Actions runs for the June 2026 month-to-date window through 2026-06-28T23:04:35Z; divided by elapsed month ratio 0.932050540123 = 107.56 projected minutes. GitHub standard runners are free for public repositories, so projected cost is 0.

services.usageSource: Cloudflare API read from local .env: /accounts/:account_id/subscriptions and /accounts/:account_id/workers/scripts; sanitized output only.
services.calculation: Cloudflare API returned Cloudflare Free Plan and 8 current Workers. The new PDF report Worker projects the account to 9 Workers, below the Workers Free allowance of 100 Workers per account. Token verify returned 401, but the account/subscription/Workers reads used for this proof succeeded.

services.usageSource: Versioned publication-evidence.json files for current Workers plus planned worker-relatorios smokes: GET /health, GET /contract and POST /reports/pdf.
services.calculation: 8 smoke requests are already represented by versioned publication evidence. The new worker-relatorios smoke adds three low-volume requests per smoke cycle (/health, /contract and POST /reports/pdf). A conservative 500 requests/month projection stays below 100,000/day * 30 days = 3,000,000 requests/month on Workers Free.

services.service: Browser Run Quick Actions browser hours
services.usageSource: Cloudflare API script settings read found 0 existing Browser Run bindings before worker-relatorios; smoke will measure X-Browser-Ms-Used from POST /reports/pdf.
services.calculation: Account is on Cloudflare Free Plan, 0 existing Workers have Browser Run bindings, and worker-relatorios v1 uses Browser Run Quick Actions through env.BROWSER.quickAction("pdf", ...) instead of REST API or long-lived browser sessions. The POST /reports/pdf endpoint is bearer-token protected, the smoke captures X-Browser-Ms-Used, and the operational projection is capped at 5 minutes/day, below the Browser Run Free allowance of 10 minutes/day.

services.usageSource: Static SQL inventory: find aneety-platform/apps -path */db-*/*.sql -type f; 21 SELECT statements across current D1 banco modules; no new D1 use in worker-relatorios v1.
services.calculation: Conservative projection for one daily full D1 gate pass across current banco modules: 21 SELECT statements over tiny seed/fixture data rounded up to 120 rows read/day, below the Workers Free D1 allowance of 5,000,000 rows read/day. worker-relatorios v1 adds no D1 usage.

services.usageSource: Static SQL inventory: find aneety-platform/apps -path */db-*/*.sql -type f; 39 INSERT, 12 UPDATE, 48 CREATE and 43 DROP statements across current D1 banco modules; no new D1 use in worker-relatorios v1.
services.calculation: Conservative projection for one daily full D1 gate pass across current banco modules: 39 INSERT + 12 UPDATE + 48 CREATE + 43 DROP statements = 142 potential written/DDL operations/day, below the Workers Free D1 allowance of 100,000 rows written/day. worker-relatorios v1 adds no D1 usage.

services.usageSource: D1 gate design creates one ephemeral database per validation and deletes it in cleanup; current SQL fixtures are tiny demo records and worker-relatorios v1 adds no storage.
services.calculation: Conservative storage projection for the remote D1 validation gate is 0.001 GB/account for ephemeral validation databases and tiny seed/fixture rows, below the Workers Free D1 total storage allowance of 5 GB. worker-relatorios v1 adds no R2, KV, Queue, D1 or persistent PDF storage.
